description: A Japanese dish of very fresh, carefully sliced raw seafood or, less commonly, other raw meats. It is served without rice and is valued for texture, freshness, and clean presentation.
preparation_steps: Select seafood that is appropriate and handled for raw consumption.
Chill and trim the seafood carefully.
Arrange neatly on a plate with garnishes.
Serve immediately with condiments on the side.

health_advice.benefits: Often high in protein and, depending on the fish, can provide omega-3 fats.
health_advice.risks: Raw seafood carries food safety risks if sourcing, storage, or handling is poor. Some fish may also be high in mercury.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Choose reputable establishments or trusted suppliers and eat raw seafood promptly and cold.

dietary_notes.note: Naturally non-vegetarian. It can be gluten-free if served without standard soy sauce or with gluten-free tamari.

allergens: Soy if served with soy sauce
